chunks.text: Constance Chang-Hasnain is John R. Whinnery Chair Professor Emerita of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at University of California, Berkeley. She earned her Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from U.C. Berkeley in 1987, her M.S. in Electrical Engineering from U.C. Berkeley in 1984, and her B.S. in Electrical Engineering from U.C. Davis in 1982.

Constance Chang-Hasnain's research area is Physical Electronics (PHY). She has received numerous honors including the Nick Holonyak Jr. Medal for Semiconductor Optoelectronics Technologies in 2024, Welker Award in 2022, ECE Distinguished Alumni Award in 2019, and National Academy of Inventors Fellow in 2018.

Additional honors include Okawa Prize in 2018, National Academy of Engineering Member in 2018, UNESCO Medal for Nanosciences in 2015, Quantum Device Award in 2014, Pan Wen-Yuan Foundation Award in 2013, IEEE David Sarnoff Award in 2011, and Max Planck Research Award in 2009.

Further recognition includes Guggenheim Fellow in 2009, Microoptics Award in 2009, Humboldt Research Award in 2009, NSSEF Fellow in 2008, Nick Holonyak Jr. Award in 2007, NAE Gilbreth Lectureship in 2005, IEEE Photonics Society Streifer Award in 2003, and James H. McGraw Award in 1999.

Earlier honors include IEEE Fellow in 1997, Optica Fellow in 1997, NSF Presidential Faculty Fellow in 1994, Sloan Research Fellow in 1994, IEEE Photonics Society Distinguished Lecturer in 1994, Packard Fellow in 1992, and IEEE-HKN Outstanding Young Professional Award in 1991.
